# Step 5 ã Multi-Mode Reading

> **Milestone**: M1.4 | **Epic**: E1.4.2 | **Feature**: F1.4.2.1
> **Depends on**: Step 1 (warmup), Step 3 (gesture polish on reading screen)
> **Skills to load**: `flutter-managing-state`, `riverpod-providers`, `riverpod-codegen-and-hooks`, `flutter-animating-apps`, `flutter-building-layouts`

Load shared context: [M1.4 reading polish context](../instructions/m1.4-reading-polish-context.instructions.md)

---

## Task

Add sentence-mode and paragraph-mode reading views alongside the existing RSVP word-at-a-time mode. Create a mode switcher that allows users to toggle between modes with a crossfade transition (respecting reduced-motion). The current RSVP mode remains the default.

This is the most complex step ã it adds a new Riverpod provider, two new view widgets, a mode switcher, and integrates them into the reading screen.

## Files to Create

### 1. `lib/features/reading/providers/reading_mode_provider.dart`

```dart
import 'package:riverpod_annotation/riverpod_annotation.dart';

part 'reading_mode_provider.g.dart';

/// The available reading modes.
enum ReadingMode {
  /// Word-at-a-time RSVP with ORP ã the default.
  rsvp,

  /// Full sentence displayed at once, advancing sentence by sentence.
  sentence,

  /// Full paragraph displayed, advancing paragraph by paragraph.
  paragraph,
}

/// Provides the current reading mode. Auto-disposed when
/// the reading screen is unmounted.
@riverpod
class ReadingModeNotifier extends _$ReadingModeNotifier {
  @override
  ReadingMode build() => ReadingMode.rsvp;

  /// Switch to a new reading mode.
  void setMode(ReadingMode mode) {
    state = mode;
  }

  /// Cycle to the next mode: rsvp ã sentence ã paragraph ã rsvp.
  void cycleMode() {
    state = ReadingMode.values[
      (state.index + 1) % ReadingMode.values.length
    ];
  }
}
```

**After creating this file, run `dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs` to generate the `.g.dart` file.**

### 2. `lib/features/reading/widgets/sentence_mode_view.dart`

```dart
/// Displays the current sentence in full, centered in the viewport.
///
/// Advances to the next sentence when the user taps or when the
/// per-word timer reaches the end of the sentence.
class SentenceModeView extends ConsumerWidget {
  const SentenceModeView({
    super.key,
    required this.words,
    required this.currentIndex,
    required this.letterSpacing,
    required this.wordSpacing,
    required this.fontFamily,
    required this.fontSize,
  });

  /// All words in the current document/slice.
  final List<String> words;

  /// The current word index (from WordTimerState).
  final int currentIndex;

  /// Extra letter spacing from accessibility settings.
  final double letterSpacing;

  /// Extra word spacing from accessibility settings.
  final double wordSpacing;

  /// Font family from config.
  final String fontFamily;

  /// Dynamic font size.
  final double fontSize;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context, WidgetRef ref) { ... }
}
```

**Behavior:**
- Find the sentence containing `currentIndex` (scan backward/forward for `.`, `!`, `?`)
- Display the full sentence as a `Text` widget
- Highlight the current word within the sentence (bold or underline ã not color-only)
- Apply `letterSpacing` and `wordSpacing` from config
- Use `RunThruTypography.body` base style with modifications
- `Semantics(label: 'Sentence mode. Current sentence: ...')`

**Sentence boundary detection:**
```dart
/// Find the start and end indices of the sentence containing [wordIndex].
(int start, int end) findSentenceBounds(List<String> words, int wordIndex) {
  int start = wordIndex;
  while (start > 0 && !_isSentenceEnd(words[start - 1])) {
    start--;
  }
  int end = wordIndex;
  while (end < words.length - 1 && !_isSentenceEnd(words[end])) {
    end++;
  }
  return (start, end);
}

bool _isSentenceEnd(String word) {
  return word.endsWith('.') || word.endsWith('!') || word.endsWith('?');
}
```

### 3. `lib/features/reading/widgets/paragraph_mode_view.dart`

```dart
/// Displays the current paragraph in full, with the current word highlighted.
///
/// Paragraphs are identified by double-newline boundaries in the original
/// document, or by every ~50 words if no paragraph breaks exist.
class ParagraphModeView extends ConsumerWidget {
  const ParagraphModeView({
    super.key,
    required this.words,
    required this.currentIndex,
    required this.letterSpacing,
    required this.wordSpacing,
    required this.fontFamily,
    required this.fontSize,
  });

  // ... same parameters as SentenceModeView ...

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context, WidgetRef ref) { ... }
}
```

**Behavior:**
- Display the paragraph containing `currentIndex`
- Highlight current word (bold, underline, or both ã not color-only)
- Scrollable if paragraph exceeds viewport height
- Use `SingleChildScrollView` with auto-scroll to keep current word visible
- Apply `letterSpacing` and `wordSpacing`
- Fallback: if no paragraph boundaries detected, chunk every 50 words

**Paragraph boundary detection:**
Use the word list + check for empty-string entries or words that start with `\n`:
```dart
/// Find start and end of the paragraph containing [wordIndex].
/// Paragraphs are separated by empty entries or explicit newline markers
/// in the word list. Falls back to 50-word chunks.
(int start, int end) findParagraphBounds(List<String> words, int wordIndex) {
  // Implementation: scan for boundaries
}
```

### 4. `lib/features/reading/widgets/mode_switcher.dart`

```dart
/// A floating button that cycles between reading modes.
///
/// Displays the current mode icon and name. Positioned in the
/// top-right area of the reading screen below the fullscreen toggle.
class ModeSwitcher extends ConsumerWidget {
  const ModeSwitcher({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context, WidgetRef ref) { ... }
}
```

**Design:**
- Compact chip-style button: icon + short label
- Icons: `Icons.text_fields` (RSVP), `Icons.short_text` (sentence), `Icons.subject` (paragraph)
- Labels: "Word", "Sentence", "Paragraph"
- `onTap`: calls `ref.read(readingModeNotifierProvider.notifier).cycleMode()`
- Touch target ãË 48dp
- `Semantics(button: true, label: 'Reading mode: ${mode.name}. Tap to switch.')`
- Colors from `RunThruTokens`, text from `RunThruTypography`

### 5. Modify `lib/screens/reading_screen.dart`

**Import new providers and widgets:**
```dart
import 'package:runthru/features/reading/providers/reading_mode_provider.dart';
import 'package:runthru/features/reading/widgets/sentence_mode_view.dart';
import 'package:runthru/features/reading/widgets/paragraph_mode_view.dart';
import 'package:runthru/features/reading/widgets/mode_switcher.dart';
```

**Watch reading mode:**
```dart
final readingMode = ref.watch(readingModeNotifierProvider);
```

**Replace the fixed `WordDisplay3D` with a mode-dependent view:**

In `_buildContent()`, replace the `WordDisplay3D` positioned widget with:

```dart
Positioned.fill(
  child: AnimatedSwitcher(
    duration: isReducedMotion(context)
        ? Duration.zero
        : const Duration(milliseconds: 300),
    child: switch (readingMode) {
      ReadingMode.rsvp => WordDisplay3D(
          key: const ValueKey('rsvp'),
          word: currentWord,
          fontSize: fontSize,
          anchorColor: anchorColor,
          fontFamily: config.fontFamily,
        ),
      ReadingMode.sentence => SentenceModeView(
          key: const ValueKey('sentence'),
          words: _words,
          currentIndex: timerState.currentIndex,
          letterSpacing: config.letterSpacing,
          wordSpacing: config.wordSpacing,
          fontFamily: config.fontFamily,
          fontSize: fontSize,
        ),
      ReadingMode.paragraph => ParagraphModeView(
          key: const ValueKey('paragraph'),
          words: _words,
          currentIndex: timerState.currentIndex,
          letterSpacing: config.letterSpacing,
          wordSpacing: config.wordSpacing,
          fontFamily: config.fontFamily,
          fontSize: fontSize,
        ),
    },
  ),
),
```

**Add mode switcher to the top controls area:**

```dart
// In the Stack, alongside the back button:
Positioned(
  top: 8,
  right: _isDesktop ? 48 : 8,
  child: const ModeSwitcher(),
),
```

---

## What NOT to Do

- Do NOT modify `WordTimerNotifier` or `WordTimerState` (Step 1 owns that)
- Do NOT modify `lib/core/orp.dart` or `lib/three_d/`
- Do NOT change the ORP display logic ã RSVP mode must render identically to before
- Do NOT persist reading mode in `AppConfig` (session-only ã auto-dispose resets on exit)
- Do NOT add per-word pacing to sentence/paragraph modes ã the timer still advances word-by-word; the VIEW just shows more context
- Do NOT forget to run build_runner after creating the provider
- Do NOT use raw `AnimatedBuilder` ã use `AnimatedSwitcher` or `ListenableBuilder`
- Do NOT create separate timers for sentence/paragraph modes

---

## Key Design Insight

The `WordTimerNotifier` continues advancing word-by-word regardless of mode. In sentence mode, the view just shows the full sentence with the current word highlighted. In paragraph mode, same thing but with the paragraph. The timer controls pacing; the view controls context. This means no timer logic changes needed.

---

## Verification

```powershell
dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs
dart analyze lib/features/reading/ lib/screens/reading_screen.dart
flutter test
```

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ReadingMode` enum exists: `rsvp`, `sentence`, `paragraph`
- [ ] `ReadingModeNotifier` is `@riverpod` with `setMode()` and `cycleMode()`
- [ ] `.g.dart` file generated via build_runner
- [ ] `SentenceModeView` displays full sentence with current word highlighted (not color-only)
- [ ] `ParagraphModeView` displays full paragraph with current word highlighted (not color-only)
- [ ] Both views apply `letterSpacing` and `wordSpacing` from config
- [ ] `ModeSwitcher` chip shows current mode icon + label
- [ ] Tapping `ModeSwitcher` cycles modes: rsvp ã sentence ã paragraph ã rsvp
- [ ] Mode switch uses `AnimatedSwitcher` with crossfade
- [ ] `AnimatedSwitcher` duration is `Duration.zero` when `isReducedMotion(context)` is true
- [ ] RSVP mode renders `WordDisplay3D` identically to before
- [ ] Word timer continues advancing in all modes ã no timer logic changes
- [ ] All public symbols have dartdoc comments
- [ ] All colors from `RunThruTokens`, all text from `RunThruTypography`
- [ ] All touch targets ãË 48dp
- [ ] `Semantics` labels on mode switcher
- [ ] `dart analyze` ã zero issues
- [ ] Existing tests pass without regression

---

## You May Refuse If

- `WordDisplay3D` has been refactored or moved since the shared context was written
- `AppConfig` does not have `letterSpacing` / `wordSpacing` (Step 4 incomplete ã skip spacing and use defaults)
- The reading screen's `_buildContent` method structure has changed significantly

List the ambiguities instead of guessing.
